title: jinx
text: R package powering R-Ladies GitHub organization bot. Deployed GitHub App (jinx[bot]) via GitHub Actions workflows.

title: jinx
text: R package (TypeScript/Probot) — R-centric org, maintainability GitHub App identity via actions/create-github-app-token@v1 /jinx commands issue comments trigger commands.yml workflow Scheduled workflows handle recurring tasks (reports, sync, monitoring) 16 modules across 50 R source files, 24 workflows, 25 templates

title: jinx
text: workflows use actions/create-github-app-token@v1 repo secrets (variables): Always reference secrets.JINX_APP_ID, never vars.JINX_APP_ID.

title: jinx
text: code comments except workaround explanations tidyverse style, roxygen2 docs testthat describe/structure Use cli:: console output (cli_alert_*, cli_abort, cli_h2) Use cli::format_inline() string interpolation instead glue::glue() Use sprintf() technical strings (GraphQL queries, regex patterns) Keep paste() data operations (vector collapsing, auth headers) Internal functions get @keywords internal @noRd Hash-pipe (#|) chunk options vignettes

title: jinx
code: R/                    # 50 source files, organized by module tests/testthat/       # describe/it tests, 330+ passing inst/config/          # YAML configs (teams, review rules, labels, events, conferences, languages) inst/templates/       # Markdown templates with <KEY> placeholders inst/translations/    # i18n: en, es, pt, fr inst/schemas/         # JSON Schema validation (chapter, blog, directory, global-team, cfp, event) inst/commands/        # help.md for /jinx help .github/workflows/    # 24 workflows vignettes/            # getting-started.Rmd, workflows.Rmd

title: jinx
text: Templates inst/templates/ use <KEY> placeholders replaced render_template(). i18n module extends language-specific templates inst/translations/{lang}/ fall back English.

title: jinx
text: Run via zsh ensure PATH includes /opt/homebrew/bin (pandoc, gh).
code: devtools::test()    # 330+ tests devtools::check()   # must pass with 0 errors, 0 warnings

title: jinx
text: _pkgdown.yml defines reference sections grouped module. Build : Deployed GitHub Pages via .github/workflows/pkgdown.yml.

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: jinx standard R package. GitHub Actions workflows call exported R functions. gh package handles GitHub API authentication via GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable. using GitHub App token (via actions/create-github-app-token), API calls comments appear jinx[bot].
code: Issue comment (/jinx invite ...)   -> issue_comment workflow triggers     -> installs jinx R package       -> calls jinx::parse_command() + jinx::execute_command()         -> gh::gh() API calls as jinx[bot]           -> reply comment posted

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: Go Settings > Developer settings > GitHub Apps > New org Name: jinx Repository: Issues (R/W), Pull Requests (R/W), Contents (Read) Organization: Members (R/W), Administration (Read) Events: issue_comment, issues, pull_request Install R-Ladies org

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: jinx repo (org-wide): Variable JINX_APP_ID – GitHub App’s ID Secret JINX_PRIVATE_KEY – GitHub App’s private key Additional secrets specific modules:

title: Getting Started with jinx
Getting Started with jinx
text: jinx responds commands posted issue comments repo installed. Commands start /jinx followed action: Commands parsed parse_command() dispatched execute_command():
code: /jinx invite @username to website /jinx offboard @username from blog /jinx report weekly /jinx chapter-setup Berlin Germany /jinx help library(jinx)  cmd <- parse_command("/jinx invite @octocat to website") cmd #> $action #> [1] "invite" #> $username #> [1] "octocat" #> $team #> [1] "website"

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: jinx organized modules, handling specific domain:

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: jinx loads configuration YAML files inst/config/: teams.yml – team definitions roles, repos, notification channels review-rules.yml – PR review assignment labeling rules labels.yml – file path label mappings

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: Templates live inst/templates/ markdown files <KEY> placeholders. Team-specific extras live inst/templates/teams/: Templates rendered via render_template() replaces <KEY> placeholders values named list.
code: inst/templates/   global-team-onboarding.md      # Base onboarding template   global-team-offboarding.md     # Base offboarding template   chapter-setup.md               # Chapter setup checklist   teams/     website.md                   # Website team extras     blog.md                      # Blog team extras     ...

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: Add workflow R-Ladies repo automated PR review:
code: # .github/workflows/jinx-review.yml name: PR Review on:   pull_request:     types: [opened, reopened, synchronize] jobs:   review:     uses: rladies/jinx/.github/workflows/pr-review.yml@main     secrets: inherit

title: Getting Started with jinx
text: See vignette("workflows") step--step guides admin workflow.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Via issue comment: happens: gt_invite() sends org invitation user tracking issue created team-specific onboarding checklist user accepts, gt_finalize_onboarding() adds appropriate team(s) repos tracking issue closed Programmatically: Checking pending invitations:

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Via issue comment: happens: gt_create_offboarding() opens offboarding tracking issue issue lists cleanup steps (remove team, repos, external services) complete, gt_finalize_offboarding() removes org access closes issue Programmatically:

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Via issue comment: happens: create_chapter_setup() opens setup tracking issue issue contains checklist: create repos, add organizers, configure Meetup, set social media create_chapter() generates chapter JSON directory create_chapter_json_pr() opens PR add chapter directory Updating existing chapter:
code: /jinx chapter-setup Berlin Germany /jinx chapter-update Berlin Germany

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Scheduled workflow runs periodically via check_chapter_health(). Via issue comment: happens: Reads chapter activity data (commits, issues, events) Classifies chapters active, quiet, inactive Posts summary recommendations Outreach inactive chapters:

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Via issue comment: happens: create_blog_entry() fetches metadata URL Creates YAML entry opens PR blog repo post_blog_checklist() adds review checklist PR

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Runs check_blog_links() find broken URLs across blog entries.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: auto_merge_pending() runs schedule merges blog PRs whose publish date arrived. triggered website-merge-pending.yml workflow.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: blog post merges, announce across platforms: Via issue comment: happens: announce_post() reads post’s YAML front matter create_announcement_message() builds message hashtags Posts Bluesky (post_bluesky()), Mastodon (post_mastodon()), LinkedIn (li_post_write()), newsletter (send_newsletter()) platform posted independently – failures one block others.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Via issue comment: Programmatically:
code: /jinx report weekly /jinx report monthly report <- generate_report(type = "weekly") publish_report(report)

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: happens: generate_gha_dashboard() queries workflow run status across org repos publish_gha_dashboard() posts formatted status table GitHub issue Scheduled workflow runs weekly via gha-dashboard.yml.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Scheduled workflow runs weekly via airtable-sync.yml. Programmatically: Syncs directory entries global team data Airtable GitHub. Creates PRs changes detected.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Fetches pending invitees Airtable sends Slack workspace invitations. Use dry_run = TRUE (default) preview sending. RSS feed integration: Subscribes Slack channels R-Ladies blog RSS feed.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: Validating directory PRs: validate_directory_pr() function runs automatically PRs modify directory entries. checks: YAML schema compliance Filename conventions (validate_entry_filename()) Image optimization (crop_directory_image(), optimize_image()) Social handle verification (verify_social_handles())

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: List contributors repo: Update contributors list: Org-wide contributor stats: Automated greetings: welcome_contributor() posts welcome comment first-time PRs thank_contributor() posts thank-PRs merged triggered welcome-contributor.yml thank-contributor.yml workflows.
code: /jinx contributors jinx /jinx contributors update jinx /jinx contributors org

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: PR review runs automatically pull requests via reusable workflows. happens: review_pr() applies rules review-rules.yml Reviewers assigned based file paths team ownership Labels applied based labels.yml mappings check_pr_naming() enforces branch title conventions See vignette("getting-started") add PR review repos.

title: Workflow Reference for R-Ladies Org Admins
text: gt_remind_stale() scans onboarding/offboarding issues gone stale posts reminder comments.

code: @Manual{,   title = {jinx: R-Ladies GitHub Organization Management},   author = {{R-Ladies Global}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/rladies/jinx}, }

title: R-Ladies GitHub Organization Management
text: R-Ladies GitHub organization management bot. R package deployed via GitHub Actions registered GitHub App identity (jinx[bot]).

title: R-Ladies GitHub Organization Management
text: Onboarding/Offboarding - Automate adding/removing global team members across org repos PR Review Automation - Auto-assign reviewers, label PRs, enforce conventions Report Generation - Org-wide activity health reports Issue Commands - Interactive /jinx commands issue comments

title: R-Ladies GitHub Organization Management
text: jinx standard R package. GitHub Actions workflows call exported R functions. gh package handles GitHub API authentication automatically via GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable. using GitHub App token (via actions/create-github-app-token), API calls comments appear jinx[bot].
code: Issue comment (/jinx invite ...)   → issue_comment workflow triggers     → installs jinx R package       → calls jinx::parse_command() + jinx::execute_command()         → gh::gh() API calls as jinx[bot]           → reply comment posted

what: Announce a blog post across multiple platforms — announce_post
title: Announce a blog post across multiple platforms — announce_post
text: Reads YAML front matter blog post, creates announcement message, posts selected platforms.

title: Announce a blog post across multiple platforms — announce_post
code: announce_post(   post,   platforms = c("bluesky", "linkedin", "mastodon"),   newsletter = TRUE )

title: Announce a blog post across multiple platforms — announce_post
text: post File path blog post (markdown YAML front matter). platforms Character vector platforms post . Defaults : "bluesky", "linkedin", "mastodon". newsletter Logical, whether also send newsletter. Defaults TRUE.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: Initial release jinx R package R-Ladies GitHub organization management.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: gt_invite(), gt_check_invitations(), gt_finalize_onboarding() onboarding new global team members. gt_create_offboarding(), gt_finalize_offboarding() offboarding. gt_remind_stale() stale issue reminders.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: announce_post() cross-posts blog announcements Bluesky, LinkedIn, Mastodon, newsletter. Platform integrations: post_bluesky(), post_mastodon(), li_post_write(), send_newsletter(). Utilities: create_announcement_message(), short_url(), tags2hash(), random_emoji().

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: validate_directory_pr(), validate_directory_entries(), validate_entry_filename() directory entry validation. verify_social_handles() social media handle verification. crop_directory_image(), optimize_image() image processing.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: create_blog_entry(), validate_blog_entry() blog content management. check_blog_links() link validation. post_blog_checklist() automated PR review checklists.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: create_chapter(), create_chapter_setup(), create_chapter_update() chapter lifecycle management. create_chapter_json_pr() directory integration. check_chapter_health() activity monitoring.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: generate_report(), publish_report() activity reports. report_chapter_health() chapter-specific reporting. format_report_markdown() report rendering.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: review_pr(), check_pr_naming() automated PR review. Configurable rules via inst/config/review-rules.yml.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: sync_directory_airtable(), sync_global_team_airtable() bidirectional sync Airtable GitHub.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: auto_merge_pending() date-based PR auto-merge. greet_contributor() contributor interaction.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: monitor_chapter_status() activity classification. prepare_inactivity_emails() outreach inactive chapters.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: send_slack_invites() community Slack onboarding. subscribe_slack_rss() blog feed integration.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: generate_gha_dashboard(), publish_gha_dashboard() org-wide GitHub Actions workflow status reporting.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: list_contributors(), list_org_contributors() contributor tracking. format_contributors(), update_contributors_list() display maintenance. welcome_contributor(), thank_contributor() automated greetings.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: /jinx issue comment interface via parse_command() execute_command(). 16 commands: invite, offboard, announce, validate-directory, chapter-health, blog-add, blog-check-links, report, chapter-setup, chapter-update, gha-dashboard, contributors, remind, help.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: list_chapter_events() queries Meetup Pro chapter events. sync_chapter_events() fetches events across configured chapters. create_event_summary(), publish_event_summary() reporting. Meetup GraphQL API integration via Meetup Pro.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: collect_chapter_activity(), collect_contributor_growth() data collection. compute_activity_trends() ASCII sparklines trend visualization. generate_analytics_dashboard(), publish_analytics_dashboard() monthly org-wide analytics.

title: jinx 0.1.0
text: list_open_cfps(), create_cfp_issue(), check_cfp_deadlines() CFP tracking via GitHub Issues. recommend_speaker(), list_speaker_recommendations() speaker management. generate_conference_report() coordination summaries.

title: jinx 0.1.0
